surface_verification.runner: uv run --with pytest python -m pytest integration-tests/test_aggregate_triage_fanout.py shared/tests/test_aggregate_triage_fanout_dedup.py -q -p no:cacheprovider
surface_verification.justification: Behavior is a Claude Code Stop hook (Python CLI). The fan-out integration test drives the REAL hook script across all 12 plugin roots in a git-backed project; cli surface, tests_run=7. No web/API/UI/store/SSE surface — the hook regenerates a local gitignored derived cache, not consumed by any client.

behaviors.behavior: N fan-out invocations (same Stop, session) -> exactly ONE regen, the other 11 emit the dedup-skip diag (register-everywhere hook + claim guard COMPOSE under a real 12-plugin fan-out)
behaviors.evidence: integration test_aggregate_triage_fanout.py::test_aggregate_triage_fanout_regenerates_once + ::test_aggregate_triage_fanout_concurrent_one_regen (real hook across all 12 plugin roots, sequential + parallel, 1 regen + 11 skips); unit test_fanout_dedup_regenerates_once PASSED

behaviors.behavior: claim is per (event, session): a second session regenerates independently
a single firing still regenerates (no regression to existing write-runtime behavior)
a non-Shipwright / pass-path invocation no-ops and never creates the claim
a failed regen (exception or non-zero rc) RELEASES the claim so a sibling/later stop retries (does not starve the event)
behaviors.evidence: test_aggregate_triage_fanout_dedup.py::test_failed_regen_releases_claim PASSED (runtime/ blocked as a file -> mkdir raises -> claim released -> retry after unblock regenerates)

behaviors.behavior: a genuinely-later Stop (>30s) re-arms and regenerates again
behaviors.evidence: no new TTL logic in this diff; the 30s-TTL re-arm is owned and proven by shared/tests/test_event_once.py
